model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=242 dy=-318 conf=1.00 -> (426,108)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click on the "Beta" project card.
The current crosshair is at (426, 108).
The Beta project card is the second card in the first row.
Let's find the center of the Beta project card.
The card extends roughly from x=271 to x=501 and y=183 to y=373.
Center of Beta card: x ≈ (271 + 501) / 2 = 386, y ≈ (183 + 373) / 2 = 278.
Current crosshair position: (426, 108).
dx = 386 - 426 = -40
dy = 278 - 108 = 170
Let's refine the coordinates.
The crosshair is at (426, 108), which is near the top center, close to the red plus sign.
The Beta card center:
Left edge: ~271
Right edge: ~501
Top edge: ~183
Bottom edge: ~373
Center X: (271+501)/2 = 386
Center Y: (183+373)/2 = 278
dx = 386 - 426 = -40
dy = 278 - 108 = 170
Confidence: 1.0 since it's a clear target.
